ref: No one in my master's house is more important than I am. The only thing he hasn't given me is you, and that's because you are his wife. I won't sin against God by doing such a terrible thing as this.”
text: M kpa mpɔɔn lichiln limina ponn ni ke m ŋa maah ban pu na, see sin upuu baanja le maa ŋmaa ŋa nibaa aa chee. Ba ŋa aa ban ke m tun kidagook aatuln li ye titunwanbir na Uwumbɔr aanimbiin ni?”
translated: If I had known that the sun would shine like this, I would not have let my children go out, because now the rain has soaked them and they are shivering. Who will go and call them so that we can sit together and ask God why the weather has changed like this?

ref: Lamech and Zillah had a son named Tubal Cain who made tools out of bronze and iron. They also had a daughter, whose name was Naamah.
text: Sila mu nan dek ki ma Tubal‑Kainn, uma le nan ye uchaa. Naama le nan ye Tubal‑Kainn aaninkpan.
translated: They say that Tubal-Cain was the first to work with metal. His sister was Naamah.

ref: God said, “I command the earth to give life to all kinds of tame animals, wild animals, and reptiles.” And that's what happened.
text: Uwumbɔr nan ki bui ke, “Tiwakor, ni ipeel, ni tiwan ni mɔmɔk baar na, ni ye nisakpiin, ni niwaatiir na, naan ki li bi kitiŋ pu.” Le ni ŋa kina.
translated: The Creator who created us said, “Tiwakor, you and your children, and your children’s children, and your descendants, and your future generations, will all live in this land.” And it is so.

ref: Because of Sarai, the Lord struck the king and everyone in his palace with terrible diseases.
text: Tɔ, ubɔr aah nan gaa Sarai, Abram chee na, le Uwumbɔr nan cha iween i bir na lir u ni waachiln aanib pu.
translated: So, after Abram had lived in Canaan for ten years, Sarai his wife took her Egyptian maidservant Hagar and gave her to her husband to be his wife.

ref: She had a second son and named him Simeon, because she said, “The Lord has heard that my husband doesn't love me.”
text: U nan ki dek lipuul ki ki ma ubijabo, ki bui ke, “Uwumbɔr ŋun ke nchal aa gee mi, le u ki tii mi ubijabo wee mu.” Nima pu na, u nan yin u ke Simeonn.
translated: He took the child in his arms, praised God, and said, “Now, Sovereign Lord, you may let your servant depart in peace, as you have promised.” And at that moment, he was called Simeon.

ref: But Jacob said, “Promise me your birthrights, here and now!” And that's what Esau did.
text: Jakob nan bui u ke, “Pue puu tipuur ke aa ga di tininkpir ngbaan tii mi.” Esɔ nan puu tii Jakob le ki di tininkpir ngbaan kooh tii u.
translated: Jakob said to his wife, “Bring me some of that red stew.” So his wife brought Jacob some of the red stew.

ref: So Judah said, “What will we gain if we kill our brother and hide his body?
text: Le Juda bui biken ke, “Ti yaa ku tinaal ki di bɔr kan, ni ga li kpa ba aanyoor?
translated: And Judah said to them, “What will we gain if we kill our brother and hide his blood?

ref: While there he met the daughter of Shua, a Canaanite man. Judah married her,
text: Nima chee le Juda nan kan Kanaann aatiŋ ni aapiibo ubaa, bi yin ute ke Sua. Le u din u,
translated: When the people of Judah left Canaan and came to live here, they found the Sua people already here. And then,

ref: Abraham named that place “The Lord Will Provide.” And even now people say, “On the mountain of the Lord it will be provided.”
text: Abraham nan yin nima chee ke, “Uwumbɔr le tii.” Ki nan saa din binib beenin len ke, “Uwumbɔr aajool paab le u tii.”
translated: Abraham said to his son, “God will provide.” And the boy replied to his father, “God himself will provide the lamb.”

ref: Jacob soon became rich and successful. He owned many sheep, goats, camels, and donkeys, as well as a lot of slaves.
text: Kina le Jakob nan ŋa mpɔɔndaan ki kpa tiwakor, ni binaagbiib, ni laakumii tiib, ni ibon sakpen.
translated: Jakob’s mother gave him a hoe, a cutlass, a cooking pot, and a basket.

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Teer ke aa nan puu tipuur tii mi ke aa ga li kii ki ter mi tiwan mɔmɔk ni, ki ga cha m maa pɔr, le n‑yaabitiib ga li wiir ke litambol li dɔ limɔɔl aagbaan na, le ubaa aan ŋmaa kahn bi.”
translated: The time has come for us to rise up as one, for if we remain divided, our enemies will continue to trample upon us, and our children will have no future.

ref: Because of Joseph, the Lord began to bless Potiphar's family and fields.
text: Josef pu na, ki nyan n‑yoonn ngbaan ki joo cha, Uwumbɔr nan ŋa tinyoor ŋa uninkpel ngbaan do, ni waawan, ni waasar mɔmɔk pu.
translated: Joseph said, “When the time comes for me to die, God will take my spirit and give it to that woman, and she will bear a child, and she will name it after me.”

ref: As long as the earth remains, there will be planting and harvest, cold and heat; winter and summer, day and night.
text: Dulnyaa aah ga li bi cha na, liwanbuul ni liwancheel aayoonn ga li bi la. Le tiwoor, ni kitotoŋ, ni unaalan, ni kitakuuk, ni nwiin pu, ni kinyeek ga li bi.”
translated: The child who is not yet born, the midwife and the one who will deliver the baby are already known. The day, the hour, the month, the week, the minute, the second are all known.

ref: May he bless you with the land he promised Abraham, so that you will take over this land where we now live as foreigners.
text: Uwumbɔr ŋa tinyoor ŋa aa ni aayaabitiib pu, ke waah ŋa tinyoor ŋa aayaaja Abraham pu pu na, aan ki cha aa gaa kitiŋ ki u nan di tii aayaaja Abraham aan aa bi ki ponn ni na.”
translated: The man who was not in the covenant, when the one who was in the covenant with Abraham came, he could not enter the covenant of Abraham unless he was circumcised.

ref: Dishan's sons were Uz and Aran.
text: Us ni Arann nan ye binibaam la, le Disann nan ye biyaaja.
translated: If it is Arann that is the elder, then Disann is the younger.

ref: This completes the list of Noah's descendants. After the flood their descendants became nations and spread all over the world.
text: Binib bimina mɔmɔk nan ye Nowa aayaabitiib la, bi mɔmɔk ni baanibol. Nnyun aah jin dulnyaa wee ki jer na, ŋinibol ŋimɔk bi dulnyaa wee ni dandana na mɔk ye Nowa aayaabitiib la.
translated: All the people who were in Noah’s time, all of them were evil. Water came and destroyed the earth, and the people who were on the earth doing evil were destroyed in Noah’s time.

ref: Hamor and Shechem liked what was said.
text: Hamor ni ujapɔɔn Sekem nan kii tii bi.
translated: The woman gave birth to Sekem and he is still alive.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Jakob mu nan cha, le Uwumbɔr aatuuntiib tooh u nsan ni.
translated: Jakob and his children, when God created the world, He gave it to them.

ref: Joseph collected and stored up the extra grain in the cities of Egypt near the fields where it was harvested.
text: Josef nan kuun tijikaar pam ki siin ntim mɔmɔk ponn ni. Baah nan kon tijikaar ntim mumɔk ponn ni na, le u nan kuun ti bil nima chee.
translated: Josef was a very good man who did not do any bad things. Baah was also a good man who did not do bad things, but he was not as good as him.

ref: Jacob went to his father Isaac at Hebron, also called Mamre or Kiriath-Arba, where Isaac's father Abraham had lived as a foreigner.
text: Le Jakob fuu ute Aisak chee Mamre aatiŋ ni, ki bi gbɔk Hebronn chee la, nin chee Abraham ni Aisak nan bi n‑yoonn mubaa na.
translated: Jacob sent for Isaac from Mamre, which is near Hebron, where Abraham and Isaac had lived as foreigners.

ref: Then I bowed my head and gave thanks to the God my master Abraham worships. The Lord had led me straight to my master's relatives, and I had found a wife for his son.
text: le ki gbaan kitiŋ nima chee, ki pak Uwumbɔr u ye maaninkpel Abraham Aawumbɔr na, ba pu? uma le ter mi, le m di nan tuur waamaal aanib do, le ki kan bibisal tii ujapɔɔn.
translated: When the people of the world were still few, God chose Abraham to be His own, and said to him: leave your land, leave your father’s house and go to a land that I will show you.

ref: He then took the knife and got ready to kill his son.
text: le ki yoor kijuk ke u kɔr ujapɔɔn ngbaan.
translated: He went to the farm and found that his yam had been stolen.

ref: So they called the place Beersheba, because they made a treaty there.
text: Nima pu na, bi nan yin nima chee ke Beerseba, ba pu? nima chee le Abraham ni Abimelek nan puu tii tɔb.
translated: Where is it that you said we should go to Beersheba, where is it? We said that Abraham and Abimelek went there to make a covenant.

ref: and in three days the king will cut off your head. He will hang your body on a pole, and birds will come and peck at it.
text: Iwiin itaa daal kan, ubɔr ga nyan si kiyondiik ni ki ti chuu gii aayil. Le u ga di saawon di leen idɔ pu, le inyoon ga dan nan ji saawon ngbaan.”
translated: If a woman gives birth to a child and the child dies, people will say it is because she killed it and ate it. But if she gives birth to twins and they both die, people will say it is because she ate two children.

ref: “But, brother, I already have plenty,” Esau replied. “Keep them for yourself.”
text: Le Esɔ bui u ke, “Nna aabo, maah kpa pu na ŋeer a; li joo sin yaan.”
translated: The man said, “My friend, I have come to greet you; let’s go home.”
